title: Dynamical analysis of a modified Leslie-Gower Holling-type II predator-prey stochastic model in polluted environments with interspecific competition and impulsive toxicant input.
abstract: In this paper, we use a mean-reverting Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process to simulate the stochastic perturbations in the environment, and then a modified Leslie-Gower Holling-type II predator-prey stochastic model in a polluted environment with interspecific competition and pulse toxicant input is proposed. Through constructing V-function and applying <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>It</mi><msup><mrow><mrow><mover><mi>o</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></mrow></mrow><mo>'</mo></msup><mi>s</mi></math> formula, the sharp sufficient conditions including strongly persistent in the mean, persistent in the mean and extinction are established. In addition, the theoretical results are verified by numerical simulation.
